desc: Termos t√©cnicos de engenharia, processos e sistemas.

cards.def: An analysis to determine whether a project is technically, operationally, and financially practical before committing resources.
cards.ex: The feasibility study confirmed the bridge design was structurally sound and within budget constraints.
cards.tip: Sempre aparece no in√≠cio de um projeto. McKinsey frequentemente conduz feasibility studies antes de qualquer proposta.

cards.def: The uncontrolled expansion of a project's scope without corresponding adjustments to time, cost, or resources.
cards.ex: Scope creep turned a three-month software rollout into an eighteen-month ordeal.
cards.tip: Um dos maiores inimigos do PM. Combatido com formal change requests e change control boards.

cards.def: The implied cost of future rework caused by choosing a quick short-term solution over a better long-term approach.
cards.ex: Years of technical debt made the legacy system nearly impossible to scale.
cards.tip: Analogia financeira usada em tech strategy. Acumula "juros" ‚ÄĒ quanto mais espera, mais caro fica resolver.

cards.def: A point of congestion in a system that limits overall throughput and prevents reaching full potential.
cards.ex: The manual approval bottleneck was delaying every downstream engineering task by two days.
cards.tip: Termo universal ‚ÄĒ supply chain, software, processos. Theory of Constraints (TOC) de Goldratt √© o framework cl√°ssico.

cards.def: A demonstration that a proposed idea or design is feasible before full-scale investment.
cards.ex: We built a proof of concept in four weeks to validate the ML pipeline before committing the full $2M budget.
cards.tip: Abreviado POC. Frequente em pitches de inova√ß√£o e digital transformation. Reduz risk antes de go-to-market.

cards.def: The capacity of a system or business to handle growing amounts of work by adding resources proportionally.
cards.ex: The architecture was designed for scalability to support 10x user growth within two years.
cards.tip: Fundamental em tech mas tamb√©m figurativo: "Is this business model scalable?" ‚ÄĒ pergunta central em consulting.

cards.def: The amount of work completed or data processed in a given unit of time; a measure of system efficiency.
cards.ex: Optimizing the assembly line increased daily throughput by 40% without additional headcount.
cards.tip: Usado em manufacturing e software. Ligado ao conceito de capacity. Throughput ‚Č† output bruto.

cards.def: A systematic process to identify the fundamental source of a failure, rather than treating only its visible symptoms.
cards.ex: The root cause analysis revealed the outage stemmed from an undocumented third-party dependency.
cards.tip: Abreviado RCA. Ferramentas: 5 Whys, Fishbone (Ishikawa). Core em Six Sigma, Lean e incident management.

cards.def: A standard or point of reference against which performance, quality, or capabilities are measured.
cards.ex: Our latency benchmarks exceeded industry standards across all test scenarios in the third-party audit.
cards.tip: Usado como noun e verb. "Let's benchmark our cost structure against competitors" ‚ÄĒ pedido frequente em consulting.

cards.def: A relationship in which one task or component requires another to be completed before it can proceed.
cards.ex: Task 7 had an undisclosed dependency on the vendor's API that blocked the entire sprint for five days.
cards.tip: Cr√≠tico no critical path analysis. Types: Finish-to-Start (FS), Start-to-Start (SS), Finish-to-Finish (FF).

cards.def: Adherence to applicable laws, regulations, standards, or specifications relevant to the project or industry.
cards.ex: The rollout was delayed three months to meet ISO 27001 compliance requirements before go-live.
cards.tip: Cr√≠tico em projetos financeiros, sa√ļde e energia. Non-compliance = serious legal and reputational liability.

cards.def: Testing where individual modules are combined and tested as a group to expose interface defects.
cards.ex: Integration testing uncovered a critical data pipeline failure between two vendor APIs before launch.
cards.tip: Distinct from unit testing (componente isolado) e UAT (user acceptance). Ordem: unit ‚Üí integration ‚Üí system ‚Üí UAT.
